info.title: trparse
info.description:
Parse files and output to stdout parse tree data.
The tool requires a pre-built parser via trgen for a grammar
for anything other than the standard parser grammars that
are supported. To specify the grammar, you can either
be in a trgen-generated parser directory, or use the -p option.
If using positional args on the command line, a file is parsed
depending on the extension of the file name:
* `.g2` for an ANTLRv2 grammar
* `.g3` for an ANTLRv3 grammar
* `.g4` for an ANTLRv4 grammar
* `.y` for a Bison grammar
* `.rex` for a Rex grammar
* `.gram` for a pegen grammar
You can force the type of parse with
the `--type` command-line option:
* `ANTLRv4` for ANTLRv2
* `ANTLRv3` for ANTLRv3
* `ANTLRv2` for ANTLRv4
* `Bison` for Bison
* `rex` for Rex
* `pegen_v3_10` for the `Generated/` parser

options.name: --dll
options.description: Search for parser in dll with this specified name.

options.name: --no-prs
options.description: Output parse errors only. No parsing result sets.

options.name: --encoding
options.description: Set the encoding for the grammar.

options.name: --line
options.description: Include line/column information in parse tree.

options.name: --parser
options.description:
Location of pre-built parser (aka the trgen Generated/
directory)

options.name: --quiet
options.description: (Default: false) Do not output anything; only set error

options.name: -x
options.description: Read input file names from stdin.

options.name: --xf
options.description: Read input file names from file provided on command line.
